member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem
member.summary: A menu item in the Completion Menu Pane.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.ReplacementText
member.summary: When the completion item is selected, this text will be inserted into the document at the specified start index.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.DisplayTextFormatted
member.summary: This text will be displayed in the completion menu.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.DisplayText
member.summary: This text will be displayed in the completion menu.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.FilterText
member.summary: The text used to determine if the item matches the filter in the list.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.CommitCharacterRules
member.summary: Rules that modify the set of characters that can be typed to cause the selected item to be committed.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.GetExtendedDescriptionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: This task will be executed when the item is selected, to display the extended "tool tip" description to the right of the menu.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.#ctor(System.String,PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString,System.String,PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.GetExtendedDescriptionHandler,System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationRule})
member.param: When the completion item is selected, this text will be inserted into the document at the specified start index.
This text will be displayed in the completion menu. If not specified, the value will be used.
This lazy task will be executed when the item is selected, to display the extended "tool tip" description to the right of the menu.
The text used to determine if the item matches the filter in the list. If not specified the value is used.
Rules that modify the set of characters that can be typed to cause the selected item to be committed.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Completion.CompletionItem.GetCompletionItemPriority(System.String,System.Int32,PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines the completion item priority in completion list with respect to currently written text. Higher numbers represent higher priority. Highest priority item will be on top of the completion list.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
Span of text that will be replaced by inserted completion item
member.returns: Integer representing priority of the item in completion list. Negative priorities represents non-matching items.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Completion.OverloadItem
member.summary: An overload item in the Overload Menu Pane.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Completion.SlidingArrayWindow
member.summary: Datastructure that provides a window over a segment of an array, similar to , but also has a concept of the window "sliding" to always keep a selected index in view. This datastructure powers the auto-complete menu, and the window slides to provide the scrolling of the menu.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationKind
member.summary: The kind of character set modification.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationKind.Add
member.summary: The rule adds new characters onto the existing set of characters.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationKind.Remove
member.summary: The rule removes characters from the existing set of characters.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationKind.Replace
member.summary: The rule replaces the existing set of characters.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationRule
member.summary: A rule that modifies a set of characters.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationRule.Kind
member.summary: The kind of modification.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.CharacterSetModificationRule.Characters
member.summary: One or more characters.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsole
member.summary: Console abstraction, mainly for testability. In the real application it will be the System.Console APIs.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsole.InitVirtualTerminalProcessing
member.summary: Enables ANSI escape codes for controlling the terminal. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/console/console-virtual-terminal-sequences

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsoleX.Write(PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsole,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Boolean)
member.param: Value to be written to console.
HideCursor() is surprisingly slow, don't use it unless we're rendering something large. The issue mainly shows when e.g. repeating characters by holding down a key (e.g. spacebar),

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress.ConsoleKeyInfo
member.summary: The key press as reported by Console.ReadKey

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress.ObjectPattern
member.summary: A tuple that represents the key press. Intended to be pattern matched, e.g. (A) or (Ctrl, A) or (Ctrl | Shift, A). It's either or (, ).

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress.PastedText
member.summary: Text that was pasted as a result of this key press.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress.MapInputEscapeSequence(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.ConsoleKeyInfo})
member.summary: On Linux, .NET doesn't map all the ANSI escaped inputs into ConsoleKeyInfos. Map some of the missing ones here.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress.ReadRemainingKeys(PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsole,System.ConsoleKeyInfo)
member.summary: Read any remaining key presses in the buffer, including the provided .

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.SystemConsole
member.summary: Implementation of that uses the normal APIs

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Consoles.SystemConsole.GetStdHandle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returned handle does not need to be closed (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/console/getstdhandle#handle-disposal).

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Documents.Document
member.summary: A Document represents the input text being typed into the prompt. It contains the text being typed, the caret/cursor positions, text selection, and word wrapping.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Documents.Document.currentText
member.summary: Cached ToStringed current stringBuilder content. This is optimization for GetText calls so they don't repeatedly ToString underlying stringBuilder.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Documents.Document.Caret
member.summary: The one-dimensional index of the text caret in the document text

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.Document.InsertAtCaretAsync(PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane,System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Consume an async enumerable while live updating the Document at each iteration. The streaming input is entered as a single "block" of undo/redo history.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.Document.BeginChanges(PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane,PrettyPrompt.Documents.Document.ChangeContextType)
member.summary: Accumulates changed events and invokes only one on dispose. Also takes care of history tracking (before/after).

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Documents.ReadOnlyStringBuilder
member.summary: A Document represents the input text being typed into the prompt. It contains the text being typed, the caret/cursor positions, text selection, and word wrapping.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Documents.StringBuilderWithCaret
member.summary: A Document represents the input text being typed into the prompt. It contains the text being typed, the caret/cursor positions, text selection, and word wrapping.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Documents.StringBuilderWithCaret.Caret
member.summary: The one-dimensional index of the text caret in the document text

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.Start
member.summary: Start point of the span.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.Length
member.summary: Length of the span.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.End
member.summary: End of the span.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.IsEmpty
member.summary: Determines whether or not the span is empty.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a TextSpan instance beginning with the position Start and having the Length specified with length.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.FromBounds(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new TextSpan from start and end positions as opposed to a position and length. The returned TextSpan contains the range with start inclusive, and end exclusive.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.Contains(System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether the position lies within the span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.Contains(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether span falls completely within this span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.OverlapsWith(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether span overlaps this span. Two spans are considered to overlap if they have positions in common and neither is empty. Empty spans do not overlap with any other span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.OverlapsWith(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether span overlaps this span. Two spans are considered to overlap if they have positions in common and neither is empty. Empty spans do not overlap with any other span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.Overlap(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the overlap with the given span, or null if there is no overlap.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.IntersectsWith(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether span intersects this span. Two spans are considered to intersect if they have positions in common or the end of one span coincides with the start of the other span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.IntersectsWith(System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether position intersects this span. A position is considered to intersect if it is between the start and end positions(inclusive) of this span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan.Intersection(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Returns the intersection with the given span, or null if there is no intersection.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Documents.UndoRedoHistory
member.summary: Stores undo/redo history for a single document.
member.remarks: Implementation is naive -- it just stores full snapshots in a list and undo/redo navigates through it. If tracking snapshots of the input ends up causing high memory, we can rework it.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.WordWrapping.WrapEditableCharacters(PrettyPrompt.Documents.ReadOnlyStringBuilder,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wraps editable input, contained in the string builder, to the supplied width. The caret index (as the input is a 1 dimensional string of text) is converted to a 2 dimensional coordinate in the wrapped text.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Documents.WordWrapping.WrapWords(PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString,System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Wrap words into lines of at most maxLength long. Split on spaces where possible, otherwise split by character if a single word is greater than maxLength.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.AnsiColor
member.summary: ANSI color definitions for the terminal. Each color has a different code depending on if it's applied as a foreground or background color.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.CellRenderer
member.summary: Given the text and the syntax highlighting information, render the text into the "cells" of the terminal screen.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.CellRenderer.ApplyColorToCharacters(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan},System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: This is just an extra function used by that highlights arbitrary text. It's not used for drawing input during normal functioning of the prompt.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.Start
member.summary: Start point of the span.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.Length
member.summary: Length of the span.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.End
member.summary: End of the span.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.IsEmpty
member.summary: Determines whether or not the span is empty.

member.summary: Determines whether the position lies within the span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.Contains(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether span falls completely within this span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.OverlapsWith(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether span overlaps this span. Two spans are considered to overlap if they have positions in common and neither is empty. Empty spans do not overlap with any other span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.OverlapsWith(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether span overlaps this span. Two spans are considered to overlap if they have positions in common and neither is empty. Empty spans do not overlap with any other span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.Overlap(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the overlap with the given span, or null if there is no overlap.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.IntersectsWith(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Determines whether span intersects this span. Two spans are considered to intersect if they have positions in common or the end of one span coincides with the start of the other span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.IntersectsWith(System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether position intersects this span. A position is considered to intersect if it is between the start and end positions(inclusive) of this span.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.Intersection(PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan)
member.summary: Returns the intersection with the given span, or null if there is no intersection.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.Offset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates new span translated by some offset.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan.WithLength(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates new span with new length.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString
member.summary: Represents text with associated non-overlapping formating spans.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString.Trim
member.summary: Removes all leading and trailing white-space characters from the current string.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString.Substring(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves a substring from this instance. The substring starts at a specified character position and has a specified length.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString.Replace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns a new string in which all occurrences of a specified string in the current instance are replaced with another specified string.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormattedString.Split(System.Char)
member.summary: Splits a string into substrings based on the provided character separator.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.History.HistoryLog.unsubmittedBuffer
member.summary: In the case where the user leaves some unsubmitted text on their prompt (the latestCodePane), we capture it so we can restore it when the user stops navigating through history (i.e. they press Down Arrow until they're back to their current prompt).

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.History.HistoryLog.persistentHistoryFilepath
member.summary: Filepath of the history storage file. If null, history is not saved. History is stored as base64 encoded lines, so we can efficiently append to the file, and not have to worry about newlines in the history entries.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.History.HistoryLog.historyPath
member.summary: Indices of path through history. Sequence can contain gaps because of filtering but it should be monotonously decreasing.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.History.HistoryLog.codePane
member.summary: The currently code pane being edited. The contents of this pane will be changed when navigating through the history.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane.Document
member.summary: The input text being edited in the pane

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane.Result
member.summary: The final input text that was entered into the pane. When null, the text is still being edited.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane.WordWrappedLines
member.summary: Document text split into lines.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane.Cursor
member.summary: The two-dimensional coordinate of the text cursor in the document, after word wrapping / newlines have been processed.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CompletionPane.VerticalPaddingHeight
member.summary: Cursor + box borders.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CompletionPane.allCompletions
member.summary: All completions available. Called once when the window is initially opened

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CompletionPane.FilteredView
member.summary: An "ordered view" over that shows the list filtered by what the user has typed.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.CompletionPane.IsOpen
member.summary: Whether or not the window is currently open / visible.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.Panes.OverloadPane.IsOpen
member.summary: Whether or not the window is currently open / visible.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Prompt.#ctor(System.String,PrettyPrompt.PromptCallbacks,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsole,PrettyPrompt.PromptConfiguration)
member.summary: Instantiates a prompt object. This object can be re-used for multiple invocations of .
member.param: The filepath of where to store history entries. If null, persistent history is disabled.
A collection of callbacks for modifying and intercepting the prompt's behavior
The implementation of the console to use. This is mainly for ease of unit testing
If null, default configuration is used.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Prompt.RenderAnsiOutput(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{PrettyPrompt.Highlighting.FormatSpan},System.Int32)
member.summary: Given a string, and a collection of highlighting instructions, create ANSI Escape Sequence instructions that will draw the highlighted text to the console.
member.param: the formatting instructions containing color information for the
the width of the console. This controls the word wrapping, and can usually be .
member.returns: A string of escape sequences that will draw the
member.remarks: This function is different from most in that it involves drawing _output_ to the screen, rather than drawing typed user input. It's still useful because if users want syntax-highlighted input, chances are they also want syntax-highlighted output. It's sort of co-opting existing input functions for the purposes of output.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.IPrompt
member.summary: The main entry point of the prompt functionality. This class should be instantiated once with the desired configuration; then can be called once for each line of input.
member.remarks: We don't actually use this interface internally, but it's likely that users will want to mock the prompt as it's IO-related.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPrompt.ReadLineAsync
member.summary: Prompts the user for input and returns the result.
member.returns: The input that the user submitted

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.PromptResult
member.summary: Represents the user's input from the prompt. If the user successfully submitted text, will be true and will contain the input. If the user cancelled (via ctrl-c), will be false and will be an empty string.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.PromptResult.Text
member.summary: The current input on the prompt when the user submited the prompt or pressed the keybinding.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.PromptResult.CancellationToken
member.summary: If your user presses ctrl-c while your application is processing the user's input, this CancellationToken will be signaled (i.e. IsCancellationRequested will be set to true)

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.KeyPressCallbackResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a user's key press, when they pressed a keybinding from .

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.KeyPressCallbackResult.Output
member.summary: Any output the consuming application wants to display as a result of the keybinding.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.StreamingInputCallbackResult
member.summary: Represents input to be streamed into the prompt. Should be used in scenarios where input to be streamed is available asynchronously.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.KeyPressCallbackAsync
member.summary: A callback your application can provide to define custom behavior when a key is pressed.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text

list.item: If a non-null is returned, the prompt will be submitted. The will be returned by the current function.
If a null is returned, then the user will remain on the current prompt.

members.member:
Looks up "Callback Functions" for particular key press. The callback function will be invoked when the keys are pressed, with the current prompt text and the caret position within the text. ConsoleModifiers can be omitted if not required.
If the prompt should be submitted as a result of the user's key press, then a non-null may be returned from the function. If a null result is returned, then the user will remain on the current input prompt.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.HighlightCallbackAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Provides syntax-highlighting for input text.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.GetSpanToReplaceByCompletionAsync(System.String,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Determines which part of document will be replaced by inserted completion item. If not specified, default word detection is used.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
member.returns: Span of text that will be replaced by inserted completion item.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.GetCompletionItemsAsync(System.String,System.Int32,PrettyPrompt.Documents.TextSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Provides to auto-completion items for specified position in the input text.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
Span of text that will be replaced by inserted completion item
member.returns: A list of possible completions that will be displayed in the autocomplete menu.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.ShouldOpenCompletionWindowAsync(System.String,System.Int32,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Controls when the completion window should open. If not specified, C#-like intellisense style behavior is used.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
Key press after which we are asking whether completion list should be automatically open.
member.returns: A value indicating whether the completion window should automatically open.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.TransformKeyPressAsync(System.String,System.Int32,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Optionaly transforms key presses to another ones.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.GetOverloadsAsync(System.String,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Provides overload items for specified position in the input text if available otherwise empty collection is returned.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
member.returns: A list of possible completions that will be displayed in the autocomplete menu.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.ConfirmCompletionCommit(System.String,System.Int32,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Completion item commit can still be discarded based on current position of caret in document. This method is called only when completion item is going to be submited.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
member.returns: if the completion item should be submitted or otherwise.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.IPromptCallbacks.FormatInput(System.String,System.Int32,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.KeyPress,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Provides way to automatically format input text.
member.param: The index of the text caret in the input text
member.returns: Formatted input and new caret position.

members.member:
This method is called only once and provides list of key press patterns with "Callback Functions". The callback function will be invoked when the keys are pressed, with the current prompt text and the caret position within the text. ConsoleModifiers can be omitted if not required.
If the prompt should be submitted as a result of the user's key press, then a non-null may be returned from the function. If a null result is returned, then the user will remain on the current input prompt.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.PromptConfiguration.HasUserOptedOutFromColor
member.summary: true if the user opted out of color, via an environment variable as specified by https://no-color.org/. PrettyPrompt will automatically disable colors in this case. You can read this property to control other colors in your application.

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.PromptConfiguration.Prompt
member.summary: Formatted prompt string to draw (e.g. "> ")

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.PromptConfiguration.MinCompletionItemsCount
member.summary: How few completion items we are willing to render. If we do not have a space for rendering of completion list with

member.name: P:PrettyPrompt.PromptConfiguration.ProportionOfWindowHeightForCompletionPane
member.summary: Determines maximum verical space allocated under current input line for completion pane.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.GetHorizontalBordersWidth(PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxType,PrettyPrompt.PromptConfiguration)
member.summary: CompletionItems: Left border + marker + right padding + right border. TextLines: Left border + left padding + right padding + right border.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.VerticalBordersHeight
member.summary: Top border + bottom border.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.CornerUpperRightCell
member.summary: Character: ┐

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.CornerLowerRightCell
member.summary: Character: ┘

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.CornerUpperLeftCell
member.summary: Character: ┌

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.CornerLowerLeftCell
member.summary: Character: └

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.EdgeHorizontalCell
member.summary: Character: ─

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.EdgeVerticalCell
member.summary: Character: │

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.EdgeVerticalAndLeftHorizontalCell
member.summary: Character: ┤

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.EdgeVerticalAndRightHorizontalCell
member.summary: Character: ├

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.EdgeHorizontalAndLowerVerticalCell
member.summary: Character: ┬

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.EdgeHorizontalAndUpperVerticalCell
member.summary: Character: ┴

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.BoxDrawing.CrossCell
member.summary: Character: ┼

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.IncrementalRendering.CalculateDiffAndWriteToConsole(PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen,PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.ConsoleCoordinate,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.IConsole)
member.summary:
Given a new screen and the previously rendered screen, returns the minimum required ansi escape sequences to render the new screen.
In the simple case, where the user typed a single character, we should only return that character (e.g. the returned string will be of length 1). A more complicated case, like finishing a word that triggers syntax highlighting, we should redraw just that word in the new color. An even more complicated case, like opening the autocomplete menu, should draw the autocomplete menu, and return the cursor to the correct position.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.IncrementalRendering.CalculateDiff(PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen,PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.ConsoleCoordinate)
member.summary:
Given a new screen and the previously rendered screen, returns the minimum required ansi escape sequences to render the new screen.
In the simple case, where the user typed a single character, we should only return that character (e.g. the returned string will be of length 1). A more complicated case, like finishing a word that triggers syntax highlighting, we should redraw just that word in the new color. An even more complicated case, like opening the autocomplete menu, should draw the autocomplete menu, and return the cursor to the correct position.
This method needs to allocate string for result. If you want to just write result to console use instead.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen
member.summary: Represents characters (TextElements) rendered on a screen. Used as part of .

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen.PositionCursor(PrettyPrompt.Rendering.Screen,PrettyPrompt.Consoles.ConsoleCoordinate)
member.summary:
We have our cursor coordinate, but its position represents the position in the input string. We need to reposition both the row/column of the cursor based on how they'll be rendered to the console:
- For the row: we may only display a subset of the rows based on the current console size, so we need to adjust the row position based on the viewport start. - For the column: repositioning is needed in the case where we've rendered CJK characters. These are are "full width" characters and take up two characters on screen.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Cell
member.summary:
Represents a single cell in the console, with any associate formatting.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halfwidth_and_fullwidth_forms A character can be full-width (e.g. CJK: Chinese, Japanese, Korean) in which case it will take up two characters on the console, so we represent it as two consecutive cells. The first cell will have of 2. the trailing cell will have set to true.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Renderer
member.summary: Given our panes, actually draw them to the screen. This class mostly deals with generating Cells, which the class then processes to generate the minimal set of ANSI escape sequences to write to the screen.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.Renderer.TrimLinesToViewPortSize(PrettyPrompt.Panes.CodePane,PrettyPrompt.Row[])
member.summary: If there are too many lines of code to show in the current console window, return a subset of the lines that fit in the console window ("viewport") along with the index of the line where the viewport starts. The lines returned will always contain the line that contains the cursor.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.Row
member.summary: A row of cells. Just here for the readability of method signatures.

member.name: T:PrettyPrompt.ScreenArea
member.summary: An area of the screen that's being rendered at a coordinate. This is conceptually a UI pane, rasterized into characters.

member.name: M:PrettyPrompt.ScreenArea.#ctor(PrettyPrompt.Consoles.ConsoleCoordinate,PrettyPrompt.Row[],System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: An area of the screen that's being rendered at a coordinate. This is conceptually a UI pane, rasterized into characters.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.TextSelection.SelectionSpan.Start
member.summary: Inclusive.

member.name: F:PrettyPrompt.TextSelection.SelectionSpan.End
member.summary: Exclusive.
